title: jasminum rufohirtum
intro: Jasminum rufohirtum, also known as the golden jasmine or Bengal jasmine, is a species of flowering plant in the jasmine family, characterized by its clusters of fragrant, golden-yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties such as antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Jasminum rufohirtum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote better sleep. The plant can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is often used in traditional Indian medicine to treat a range of ailments.
